endpoints.description: Existing /api/pizzas listing — reused by the builder for name → id resolution before deep-linking. The builder picks the pizza whose `name` field (case-insensitive) matches the feature's `<item>` column and uses its `id` as the `?item=` query value.

Builder-extended /api/cart POST. Same endpoint as checkout's addToCart but the line items carry a `toppings: string[]` field per the customizer's user-selected toppings. Backend stores them on the cart row's `config.toppings` (see CheckoutDao's CartItemResponse.config.toppings). On older deployments that don't yet honor toppings, the cart line falls back to {pizza_id, size, quantity} silently — flagged here as an explicit contract expectation so the contract validator catches the regression.

TDD-style expectation — NOT a backing HTTP endpoint, but documented here so the contract surface captures the FE expectation. The atomic builder entry depends on the OmniPizza frontend honoring `/customizer?item=<pizzaId>&market=<MARKET>&language=<lang>` (web) and `omnipizza://customizer?item=<pizzaId>&market=<MARKET>&language=<lang>` (mobile). When the resolved pizza id maps to no pizza, the FE renders an empty customizer; this contract is satisfied once the FE wires the route. Until then, scenarios that exercise this entry will fail with `pizzaBuilderScreen` (mobile) or `confirmAddToCartButton` (web) not visible.
